senses.definition: plural of sham; things that are not real or genuine; fake items or acts intended to deceive
examples: The antiques were real, not shams.
He exposed a collection of shams marketed as authentic art.

senses.definition: a variant transliteration of a sword used in South Asian contexts; a blade carved for combat or ceremonial purposes
examples: The shamsheer hung beside the door as a ceremonial blade.
Historical displays showed the shamsheer used by mounted warriors.

senses.definition: a type of curved Persian sword; a sabre with a broad curved blade
examples: The shamshir swung with a swift arc.
Historical arms collections feature ornate shamshirs.

senses.definition: a man who acts as a guide or storyteller, especially in Irish or Scottish folklore; a responsible local storyteller
examples: The shamus told tales of old times by the fire.
In some communities, the shamus preserves local legends.

senses.definition: plural of shamuse, a person who amuses or entertains; entertainers or clowns
examples: The shamuses kept the crowd laughing all night.
Several shamuses performed at the festival.

senses.definition: a Chinese surname or given name; also used in names of places or groups
examples: Mr. Shan is visiting from Shanghai.
Shan mountains are known for their beauty.

senses.definition: unknown or very rare term; may refer to personal or proper noun variants; no established common sense
examples: Shanachas is listed in some historical glossaries, but usage is unclear.

senses.definition: a traditional Irish storyteller or keeper of oral history
examples: The shanachie shared legends passed down through generations.
In Gaelic culture, the shanachie character preserved memory and song.

senses.definition: plural of shanachie; multiple traditional storytellers
examples: The shanachies gathered to recount old myths.

senses.definition: plural form of shanachus, a storyteller in Irish Gaelic tradition
examples: The shanachus spoke in fluent Gaelic at the gathering.

senses.definition: a coarse, haggard person; an old or worn figure; dialect term with various meanings
examples: Old ships creaked as the shand walked the deck.

senses.definition: referring to the world or works of Thomas Shandean; not a common term in standard usage
examples: The anthology included several shandean passages.

senses.definition: plural of shandy; a drink made by mixing beer with lemonade or ginger ale
examples: We ordered two shandies on a sunny afternoon.
Shandies are refreshing in warm weather.

senses.definition: a member of a group associated with the Shandite tradition; rare or specialized usage
examples: A shandite provided a tale at the festival.

senses.definition: plural form of shandry; a type of old-fashioned transport or a haphazard manner
examples: The shandries rattled along the lane.

senses.definition: a rough, low cart or wagon; also a poor or clumsy person in some dialects
examples: The farmer pulled the shandry cart to the barn.

senses.definition: a large, old-fashioned cart or wagon drawn by humans or animals
examples: A shandrydan stood by the road waiting for travelers.

senses.definition: plural of shandrydan; multiple old-fashioned wagons
examples: The parade featured several shandrydans.

senses.definition: slang or dialect for hands; a unit of distance or measure in older usage; can refer to people who are adept at tasks
examples: Wash your hands, shands, before you eat.
He has a steady shands at card games.

senses.definition: a drink made by mixing beer with lemonade or ginger ale; also a light, mixed approach
examples: I ordered a shandy on a warm afternoon.
The team used a shandy approach to the project.

senses.definition: a specific type of shandy made with beer and a large amount of ginger beer or lemonade; historically a strong mixed drink
examples: The bar served a strong shandygaff on summer evenings.

senses.definition: plural of shandygaff; multiple shandygaffs
examples: The tavern offered several shandygaffs to choose from.

senses.definition: the practice or practice of mixing drinks (beer with lemonade) or a mixed approach; a light, blended style
examples: Shandyism in cocktails often yields refreshing results.
Some writers describe the project with a touch of shandyism.

senses.definition: a given name; also a surname in some cultures
examples: Shane spoke with a soft accent.
The author Shane published a new novel.

senses.definition: a traditional Chinese sexagenary cycle term or a name; may appear in lists of historical terms
examples: In the text, shang appears as a historical term.
